title: SkyStats
description: Hypixel SkyBlock stats tracker with 15,000+ MAU, built and maintained over three years. Rewrote the iOS client to SwiftUI and migrated the backend to MongoDB, reducing latency by 20%.

blog.subtitle: The app I learned everything on

sections.heading: The three year app
paragraphs: I built SkyStats when I was fifteen. By the time I retired it into SkyHub it had fifteen thousand people checking it every month. I did not plan for that. I did not market it. It grew because one Discord community posted a screenshot and the rest happened on its own.
Everything I know about running a real service for real users I learned from being woken up by this app.

sections.heading: The rewrite nobody saw
paragraphs: Midway through its life I rewrote the iOS client from UIKit into SwiftUI and moved the backend off a Python monolith onto a MongoDB-backed service. The visible change was a cleaner UI. The invisible change was a twenty percent drop in average request latency.
The rewrite shipped in the same update as a cosmetic redesign so that the speed gain would be attributed to the new look. This is a dirty trick and I would do it again.

sections.heading: What I kept, what I threw out
paragraphs: I kept the API scraper. I kept the theme. I threw out the networth code, which had grown into a single thousand-line file that nobody including me could read. SkyHub has the successor to that code and it is under four hundred lines and covered by tests.

sections.heading: Why I retired it
paragraphs: A product that cannot evolve is a product that is slowly dying. SkyStats could not evolve. The data model said no.
SkyHub was not a new idea. It was the same idea on a foundation that does not refuse requests.
